case_title: MARTIN LEUNG VS. CHINESE COMMUNITY HEALTH CARE ASSOCIATION et al

docket_entries.description: ANSWER TO COMPLAINT FILED BY DEFENDANT CHINESE COMMUNITY HEALTH PLAN
PROOF OF SERVICE OF MOTION FILED BY DEFENDANT CHINESE COMMUNITY HEALTH CARE ASSOCIATION
SUPPLEMENENTAL NOTICE OF AND REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE FILED BY DEFENDANT CHINESE COMMUNITY HEALTH CARE ASSOCIATION

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CHINESE COMMUNITY HEALTH CARE ASSOCIATION'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY PROCEEDINGS IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF ENTERED INTO AN AGREEMENT WITH DEFENDANT CHINESE COMMUNITY HEALTH CARE ASSOCIATION ("CCHCA") THAT GOVERNS THE TERMS OF HIS COMPENSATION. (DECLARATION OF EDWARD A. CHOW, EX. 1.) THE ARBITRATION PROVISION IN THE AGREEMENT IS ENFORCEABLE BY DEFENDANT CCHCA AS A SIGNATORY TO THE AGREEMENT. THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT IS ENFORCEABLE BY DEFENDANT CHINESE COMMUNITY HEALTH PLAN, A NON-SIGNATORY TO THE AGREEMENT THAT JOINS IN DEFENDANT CCHCA'S MOTION, THROUGH THE DOCTRINE OF EQUITABLE ESTOPPEL. THE CLAIMS PLED IN THE COMPLAINT RELATE TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPENSATION, WHICH IS GOVERNED BY THE AGREEMENT. THEREFORE, PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS ARE INTERTWINED WITH THE AGREEMENT AND EQUITABLE ESTOPPEL APPLIES. (SEE MOLECULAR ANALYTICAL SYSTEMS V. CIPHERGEN BIOSYSTEMS, INC. (2010) 186 CAL.APP.4TH 696, 706.) PURSUANT TO CAL. CODE. CIV. PROC. 1281.4, THE ENTIRE SUIT IS STAYED PENDING THE OUTCOME OF ARBITRATION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

Mtn To Compel Arb And Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Dec-05-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: YESENIA GARCIA PEREZ, ESQ., 415.984.9834, FOR DEFENDANT CHINESE COMMUNITY HEALTHCARE ASSOCIATION ET AL.; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CHINESE COMMUNITY HEALTH CARE ASSOCIATION'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings is Granted. Plaintiff entered into an agreement with Defendant Chinese Community Health Care Association ("CCHCA") that governs the terms of his compensation. (Declaration of Edward A. Chow, Ex. 1.) The arbitration provision in the agreement is enforceable by Defendant CCHCA as a signatory to the agreement. The arbitration agreement is enforceable by Defendant Chinese Community Health Plan, a non-signatory to the agreement that joins in Defendant CCHCA's motion, through the doctrine of equitable estoppel. The claims pled in the complaint relate to Plaintiff's compensation, which is governed by the agreement. Therefore, Plaintiff's claims are intertwined with the agreement and equitable estoppel applies. (See Molecular Analytical Systems v. Ciphergen Biosystems, Inc. (2010) 186 Cal.App.4th 696, 706.) Pursuant to Cal. Code. Civ. Proc. 1281.4, the entire suit is stayed pending the outcome of arbitration. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

Joinder And Or Non-Oppos To Chinese Community Hearlth Care Associations Mtn To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: YESENIA GARCIA PEREZ, ESQ., 415.984.9834, FOR DEFENDANT CHINESE COMMUNITY HEALTHCARE ASSOCIATION ET AL.; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CHINESE COMMUNITY HEALTH PLAN'S Joinder And Or Non-Opposition To Chinese Community Health Care Association's Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings is Granted. Plaintiff entered into an agreement with Defendant Chinese Community Health Care Association ("CCHCA") that governs the terms of his compensation. (Declaration of Edward A. Chow, Ex. 1.) The arbitration provision in the agreement is enforceable by Defendant CCHCA as a signatory to the agreement. The arbitration agreement is enforceable by Defendant Chinese Community Health Plan, a non-signatory to the agreement that joins in Defendant CCHCA's motion, through the doctrine of equitable estoppel. The claims pled in the complaint relate to Plaintiff's compensation, which is governed by the agreement. Therefore, Plaintiff's claims are intertwined with the agreement and equitable estoppel applies. (See Molecular Analytical Systems v. Ciphergen Biosystems, Inc. (2010) 186 Cal.App.4th 696, 706.) Pursuant to Cal. Code. Civ. Proc. 1281.4, the entire suit is stayed pending the outcome of arbitration. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

Mtn To Compel Arb And Stay Proceedings

MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant CHINESE COMMUNITY HEALTH CARE ASSOCIATION'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
IS Continued to December 5, 2013 to give Mr. Leung the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Mr. Leung must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than November 18, 2013 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be granted. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the moving and reply papers. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.
